ChartOfAccount.title: ChartOfAccount
ChartOfAccount.description: Represents a single account in the Chart of Accounts (COA), which forms the foundational structure for all financial entries. Accounts can be hierarchical, with parent-child relationships.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChartOfAccount entity.

accountCode.description: The unique code for the account, typically following a hierarchical structure (e.g., '101.10.0000').

accountName.description: The descriptive name of the account (e.g., 'Fixed Deposit Receipt (FDR)').

accountType.description: The classification of the account (e.g., Asset, Liability, Income, Expense, Contra-Asset).

normalBalance.description: Indicates whether the account typically has a debit or credit balance.

parentAccountId.description: Reference to the parent account in the Chart of Accounts, if this account is a sub-account. (Relationship: ChartOfAccount 1:N ChartOfAccount)

Employee.title: Employee
Employee.description: Represents an individual employee, who is a member of the CPF fund. This entity stores their personal and employment details relevant to CPF management and subsidiary ledger tracking.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Employee entity, typically the employee's ID number as seen in the subsidiary ledger.

name.description: The full name of the employee.

designation.description: The job designation of the employee.

dateJoined.description: The date when the employee joined the organization.

currentAddress.description: The current residential address of the employee.

permanentAddress.description: The permanent residential address of the employee.

zonalOffice.description: The zonal office or branch where the employee is based.

dateOfNomination.description: The date when the employee made their CPF nomination.

JournalEntry.title: JournalEntry
JournalEntry.description: Represents a complete and balanced financial transaction in the general ledger, serving as the header for one or more JournalEntryLineItems. All financial activities recorded in the system flow through journal entries.

id.description: Unique identifier for the JournalEntry entity.

transactionDate.description: The date when the financial transaction occurred.

particulars.description: A brief description or narrative of the transaction, often serving as the 'Particulars' field in ledgers (e.g., 'PJ-08-001').

referenceNumber.description: An optional external reference number or identifier for the transaction (e.g., invoice number, payment reference).

isPosted.description: Indicates whether the journal entry has been formally posted to the ledger, making it permanent and immutable.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the journal entry was initially created in the system.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the journal entry was last modified in the system.

JournalEntryLineItem.title: JournalEntryLineItem
JournalEntryLineItem.description: Represents an individual debit or credit line item that constitutes a part of a JournalEntry. Each line item is linked to a specific ChartOfAccount and can optionally link to subsidiary entities like Employee, InvestmentAsset, or EmployeeLoan.

id.description: Unique identifier for the JournalEntryLineItem entity.

journalEntryId.description: Reference to the parent JournalEntry this line item belongs to. (Relationship: JournalEntry 1:N JournalEntryLineItem)

accountId.description: Reference to the ChartOfAccount entry that this line item affects. (Relationship: ChartOfAccount 1:N JournalEntryLineItem)

amount.description: The monetary amount of the debit or credit entry.

entryType.description: Specifies whether the line item is a 'Debit' or 'Credit'.

employeeId.description: Optional reference to an Employee, if this transaction line directly relates to a member's fund or loan activity (e.g., contributions, loan repayments). (Relationship: Employee 1:N JournalEntryLineItem)

investmentAssetId.description: Optional reference to an InvestmentAsset, if this transaction line directly relates to an investment's acquisition, sale, or interest accrual. (Relationship: InvestmentAsset 1:N JournalEntryLineItem)

employeeLoanId.description: Optional reference to an EmployeeLoan, if this transaction line directly relates to a specific employee loan's disbursement, principal repayment, or interest application. (Relationship: EmployeeLoan 1:N JournalEntryLineItem)

InvestmentAsset.title: InvestmentAsset
InvestmentAsset.description: Represents a specific investment instrument or asset held by the CPF fund (e.g., a Fixed Deposit, Savings Certificate, or Government Bond). This entity tracks the details of each individual investment.

id.description: Unique identifier for the InvestmentAsset entity.

investmentType.description: The general type of investment (e.g., 'Fixed Deposit Receipt', 'Savings Certificate', 'Govt. Treasury Bond', 'Share', 'Mutual Fund', 'Other investment') as per the Chart of Accounts.

name.description: A specific name or identifier for the investment (e.g., 'FDR 123456 from ABC Bank', 'Bangladesh Bond Series A').

acquisitionDate.description: The date when the investment was acquired or purchased.

acquisitionCost.description: The original cost or principal amount paid for the investment.

interestRate.description: The applicable annual interest rate for the investment, if any (e.g., for FDRs, Bonds).

maturityDate.description: The maturity date of the investment, if applicable.

description.description: A detailed description or additional notes about the investment, including terms and conditions.

relatedAccountId.description: Reference to the primary ChartOfAccount for this investment asset (e.g., '101.10.0000 Fixed Deposit Receipt'). (Relationship: ChartOfAccount 1:N InvestmentAsset)

EmployeeLoan.title: EmployeeLoan
EmployeeLoan.description: Represents a specific loan provided from the CPF fund to an individual employee. This entity tracks the master details and current status of each loan.

id.description: Unique identifier for the EmployeeLoan entity.

employeeId.description: Reference to the Employee who took out this loan. (Relationship: Employee 1:N EmployeeLoan)

loanType.description: The type of loan provided (e.g., 'CPF Loan', '80% Advance CPF') as per the Chart of Accounts.

disbursementDate.description: The date when the loan amount was initially disbursed to the employee.

originalPrincipalAmount.description: The initial principal amount of the loan disbursed to the employee.

interestRate.description: The annual interest rate applicable to the loan.

terms.description: A description of the loan terms, including repayment schedule, frequency, and other conditions.

currentPrincipalOutstanding.description: The current outstanding principal balance of the loan. This value should be meticulously updated by all loan-related JournalEntryLineItems (disbursements, repayments).

status.description: The current status of the loan (e.g., 'Active', 'Repaid', 'Defaulted').

lastInterestCalculationDate.description: The last date when interest was calculated and possibly accrued for this loan, used for accurate accrual tracking.

definition.description: Global administrative role assignment. Existence of a document at this path for a user's UID grants administrative privileges. This entity is conceptual and not explicitly defined in the provided schema, representing a minimal document (e.g., { 'active': true }).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (Firebase Authentication UID) who is assigned the administrative role.

definition.description: Master list of all financial accounts, forming the foundational structure for financial entries. Admin read/write access. Includes 'parentAccountId' for hierarchical data relationships, not authorization.

params.name: accountId
params.description: The unique identifier for a Chart of Account entry.

definition.description: Master list of employees, including their personal and employment details relevant to CPF management. Admin read/write access.

params.name: employeeId
params.description: The unique identifier for an Employee.

definition.description: Details of individual investment instruments held by the CPF fund. Admin read/write access. Includes denormalized 'relatedAccountId' for data integrity and efficient filtering (not for authorization rules).

params.name: investmentAssetId
params.description: The unique identifier for an Investment Asset.

definition.description: Details of loans provided from the CPF fund to individual employees. Admin read/write access. Includes denormalized 'employeeId' for data integrity and efficient filtering (not for authorization rules on the loan document itself).

params.name: employeeLoanId
params.description: The unique identifier for an Employee Loan.

definition.description: Header for financial transactions, representing a complete and balanced entry. Admin read/write access. Uses 'isPosted' for explicit state modeling.

params.name: journalEntryId
params.description: The unique identifier for a Journal Entry.

definition.description: Individual debit or credit line items belonging to a parent JournalEntry. To ensure Authorization Independence, it MUST include the 'isPosted' status from its parent JournalEntry document as a denormalized field. It will also include 'journalEntryId', 'accountId', 'employeeId', 'investmentAssetId', and 'employeeLoanId' for data linkage, supporting QAP-compliant queries (e.g., listing all line items for a specific employee).

params.name: journalEntryId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Journal Entry.

params.name: lineItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for a Journal Entry Line Item.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to facilitate secure, scalable, and debuggable financial management for the PBS CPF Compass application. It strictly adheres to the core design principles:

1.  **Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** This is achieved primarily through strategic denormalization. For instance, `JournalEntryLineItem` documents, residing in a subcollection, will include the `isPosted` status (and potential `ownerId` if JournalEntry were user-owned) from their parent `JournalEntry` as a denormalized field. This allows security rules to evaluate authorization directly on the line item document (e.g., `resource.data.isPosted`) without needing a `get()` operation to fetch the parent `JournalEntry`. This prevents cascading rule failures, supports atomic batch writes/transactions, and makes rules simpler to write and debug.

2.  **Clarity of Intent (Debuggability):** The collection paths are logically grouped by entity, and their descriptions clearly state access patterns (e.g., 'Admin read/write'). Denormalized fields are explicitly mentioned in the descriptions, making the authorization intent transparent.

3.  **DBAC (No Custom Claims):** User roles are stored in the database. A dedicated top-level collection, `/roles_admin/{userId}`, is used to grant administrative privileges. Authorization rules will check for the existence of a document at `db.collection('roles_admin').doc(request.auth.uid)` to determine if a user is an administrator.

4.  **QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** This structure supports efficient and secure `list` operations.
    *   **Structural Segregation:** Most top-level collections (`/chart_of_accounts`, `/employees`, `/investment_assets`, `/employee_loans`, `/journal_entries`) are designed with a homogeneous security posture, primarily allowing admin-level `read` and `write`. This means a simple rule like `allow list: if isAdmin();` will retrieve all documents that the user is authorized to see, without requiring client-side filtering.
    *   **Employee-Specific Data:** While `JournalEntryLineItem`s are broadly admin-managed, the requirement for an 'Employee Subsidiary Ledger' implies employees need to view their specific financial transactions. By ensuring that any `JournalEntryLineItem` related to an employee *always* populates its `employeeId` field, employees can perform QAP-compliant queries like `db.collectionGroup('line_items').where('employeeId', '==', request.auth.uid)`. This allows secure listing of an employee's relevant transactions without `get()` calls to other documents for authorization, as `employeeId` is directly on the `resource.data`.

5.  **Invariants:** The structure supports data integrity by using clear foreign key references (e.g., `employeeId` in `EmployeeLoan`) and explicit state modeling (`isPosted` in `JournalEntry`). Timestamps (`createdAt`, `updatedAt`) are part of the `JournalEntry` schema.

**Denormalization Strategy for Authorization Independence:**
*   **`JournalEntryLineItem`:** Each `JournalEntryLineItem` document will include a denormalized `isPosted` field (copied from its parent `JournalEntry`) to enable atomic evaluation of immutability rules. This ensures that creating or updating a line item within a batch, or independently, can check the transaction's posted status without needing to `get()` the parent `JournalEntry` document.

This design prioritizes a flat, simple authorization model where rules operate on the document in context, avoiding complex `get()` dependencies and supporting efficient querying required by QAPs.
